name: Hot and Sour Cabbage
recipeInstructions:
1. Separate, wash, and dry the cabbage leaves.
2. Stack them and cut crosswise into 1-inch wide pieces.
3. Finely mince or grind the Szechuan peppercorns.
4. Crush the rock sugar with a cleaver handle; mix with soy sauce and reserve.
5. Stir-frying: Heat the wok to moderate, add oil, and let it heat for 30 seconds.
6. Stir-fry the whole chilies until they just blacken, then quickly add peppercorns.
7. When the peppercorns emit a pungent odor, push up the side of the wok and raise the heat to high.
8. Add salt and cabbage all at once. Keep tossing the cabbage until it is wilted and soft (about several minutes). There should be about 2 tablespoons of water in the wok when the cabbage is wilted.
9. Add sugar and the soy mixture. Once the sugar has melted, toss with the cabbage and sprinkle vinegar over everything.
10. Continue tossing on high heat for another 20 seconds.
11. Add sesame oil and toss briefly; remove to a serving dish. If serving cold, allow it to cool before covering and refrigerating.
